initial.prompt:
<context>
You are an expert at solving ARC-AGI (Abstraction and Reasoning Corpus) puzzles using Answer Set Programming (ASP).

ARC-AGI puzzles show input-output grid transformations. Each cell contains a color value (integer 0–9). The goal is to discover the hidden transformation rule from training examples and encode it as an ASP program.
</context>

<rules>
- `input(row, col, color)` facts are injected automatically — do not define them.
- `color(0..9)` facts are injected automatically — do not define them.
- the output predicate is always `output(row, col, color)` — do not redefine it.
- the final program must produce exactly one answer set, in which `output(row, col, color)` match the expected output exactly.
</rules>

Below are two fully worked examples.

<example_1>
<example_grid_1>
Input:
<diagram>
0 7 7
7 7 7
0 7 7
</diagram>

Output:
<diagram>
0 0 0 0 7 7 0 7 7
0 0 0 7 7 7 7 7 7
0 0 0 0 7 7 0 7 7
0 7 7 0 7 7 0 7 7
7 7 7 7 7 7 7 7 7
0 7 7 0 7 7 0 7 7
0 0 0 0 7 7 0 7 7
0 0 0 7 7 7 7 7 7
0 0 0 0 7 7 0 7 7
</diagram>
</example_grid_1>

<transformation>
The input is treated as a tile map. The output is NxM times larger than the input (where the input is N rows x M cols). Each input cell at (BR, BC) with a non-zero value causes a copy of the full input to be placed in the corresponding NxM block of the output (at rows BR*N .. BR*N+N-1, cols BC*M .. BC*M+M-1). Each input cell with value 0 produces an all-zero block at that position.
</transformation>

<asp>
% Count distinct row/col indices — equals grid dimensions for 0-indexed inputs
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
n_cols(N) :- N = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% Output is NR*NR rows and NC*NC cols (input tiled by itself)
output_cell(R, C) :-
    input(BR, BC, _), input(DR, DC, _),
    n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
    R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC.

1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

% Non-zero block: the sub-cell must equal the input value at the relative position
:- input(BR, BC, V), V != 0,
   input(DR, DC, TileColor),
   n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
   R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC,
   output(R, C, ActualColor), ActualColor != TileColor.

% Zero block: every sub-cell must be 0
:- input(BR, BC, 0),
   input(DR, DC, _),
   n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
   R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC,
   output(R, C, ActualColor), ActualColor != 0.
</asp>
</example_1>

<transformation>
The output is the same size as the input. Color-3 cells are unchanged. Non-3 cells (value 0) that are enclosed by color-3 walls — i.e., cannot reach the grid boundary via 4-connected movement through non-3 cells — are filled with color 4. All other non-3 cells remain 0.
</transformation>

<asp>
% Output has the same dimensions as the input
output_cell(R, C) :- input(R, C, _).
1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

% Grid boundary coordinates
min_row(R) :- R = #min{Row : input(Row, _, _)}.
max_row(R) :- R = #max{Row : input(Row, _, _)}.
min_col(C) :- C = #min{Col : input(_, Col, _)}.
max_col(C) :- C = #max{Col : input(_, Col, _)}.

% 4-connected adjacency offsets
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

% Non-3 cells on the grid boundary are reachable from outside
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, min_row(Rmin), R = Rmin.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, max_row(Rmax), R = Rmax.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, min_col(Cmin), C = Cmin.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, max_col(Cmax), C = Cmax.

% Flood-fill outward through non-3 cells
outside(R2, C2) :-
    outside(R1, C1), delta(DR, DC),
    R2 = R1+DR, C2 = C1+DC,
    input(R2, C2, V), V != 3.

% Color-3 cells stay 3
:- input(R, C, 3), output(R, C, Color), Color != 3.

% Outside non-3 cells stay 0
:- outside(R, C), output(R, C, Color), Color != 0.

% Interior non-3 cells (enclosed by 3s, not reachable from boundary) become 4
:- input(R, C, 0), not outside(R, C), output(R, C, Color), Color != 4.
</asp>
</example_2>

<task>

full_program:
% Determine the maximal row and column indices of the input
max_r(Rmax) :- Rmax = #max{R : input(R, _, _)}.
max_c(Cmax) :- Cmax = #max{C : input(_, C, _)}.
M = max_c + 1.                     % number of columns of the input (0‑based)

% Positions of the output grid (rows 0..max_r, columns 0..2*M‑1)
cell(R, C) :-
    R >= 0,
    R <= max_r,
    C >= 0,
    C < 2 * M.

% Copy the whole input unchanged (left block)
output(R, C, V) :-
    input(R, C, V).

% Copy the whole input shifted by M columns (right block)
output(R, C, V) :-
    input(R, C0, V),
    C0 < M,                     % C0 is a column of the input (0..M‑1)
    C = C0 + M.                 % column in the right block (M..2*M‑1)

% No two different colours may appear in the same cell
:- output(R, C, V1), output(R, C, V2), V1 != V2.

% Every cell of the output grid must have exactly one colour
:- cell(R, C), not output(R, C, _).

% No coloured cell may appear outside the defined output grid
:- not cell(R, C), output(R, C, _).

```
% Dimensions of the input grid
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
n_cols(M) :- M = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% For each input cell we create two output cells (original column and shifted by M)
output_cell(R, C) :-
    n_rows(N), n_cols(M),
    R >= 0,
    C >= 0,
    R < N,
    C < 2*M,
    input(R, C2, _),
    C = C2.
output_cell(R, C) :-
    n_rows(N), n_cols(M),
    R >= 0,
    C >= 0,
    R < N,
    C < 2*M,
    input(R, C2, _),
    C = C2 + M.

% The color of an output cell must be the color of the corresponding input cell
output(R, C, V) :-
    output_cell(R, C),
    input(R, C2, V),
    C = C2.
output(R, C, V) :-
    output_cell(R, C),
    input(R, C2, V),
    C = C2 + M.

% A cell cannot have two different colors
:- output(R, C, V1), output(R, C, V2), V1 != V2.
```

% Dimensions of the input grid
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
n_cols(M) :- M = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% Build each output cell from the corresponding input cell.
% The first copy (copy index = 0) copies the cell directly.
output_cell(R, C) :-
    n_rows(N), n_cols(M),
    R >= 0,
    C >= 0,
    R < N,
    C >= 0,
    C < 2*M,
    input(R, C2, _),
    C = C2.
% The second copy (copy index = 1) shifts the cell by M columns.
output_cell(R, C) :-
    n_rows(N), n_cols(M),
    R >= 0,
    C >= 0,
    R < N,
    C >= 0,
    C < 2*M,
    input(R, C2, _),
    C = C2 + M.

% The colour of an output cell must be the colour of the source cell.
output(R, C, V) :-
    output_cell(R, C),
    input(R, C2, V),
    C = C2.
output(R, C, V) :-
    output_cell(R, C),
    input(R, C2, V),
    C = C2 + M.

% No cell may receive two different colours.
:- output(R, C, V1), output(R, C, V2), V1 != V2.
```

<transformation>
The input is treated as a tile map. The output is NxM times larger than the input (where the input is N rows x M cols). Each input cell at (BR, BC) with a non-zero value causes a copy of the full input to be placed in the corresponding NxM block of the output (at rows BR*N .. BR*N+N-1, cols BC*M .. BC*M+M-1). Each input cell with value 0 produces an all-zero block at that position.
</transformation>

<asp>
% Count distinct row/col indices — equals grid dimensions for 0-indexed inputs
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
n_cols(N) :- N = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% Output is NR*NR rows and NC*NC cols (input tiled by itself)
output_cell(R, C) :-
    input(BR, BC, _), input(DR, DC, _),
    n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
    R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC.

1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

% Non-zero block: the sub-cell must equal the input value at the relative position
:- input(BR, BC, V), V != 0,
   input(DR, DC, TileColor),
   n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
   R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC,
   output(R, C, ActualColor), ActualColor != TileColor.

% Zero block: every sub-cell must be 0
:- input(BR, BC, 0),
   input(DR, DC, _),
   n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
   R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC,
   output(R, C, ActualColor), ActualColor != 0.
</asp>

<transformation>
The output is the same size as the input. Color-3 cells are unchanged. Non-3 cells (value 0) that are enclosed by color-3 walls — i.e., cannot reach the grid boundary via 4-connected movement through non-3 cells — are filled with color 4. All other non-3 cells remain 0.
</transformation>

<asp>
% Output has the same dimensions as the input
output_cell(R, C) :- input(R, C, _).
1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

% Grid boundary coordinates
min_row(R) :- R = #min{Row : input(Row, _, _)}.
max_row(R) :- R = #max{Row : input(Row, _, _)}.
min_col(C) :- C = #min{Col : input(_, Col, _)}.
max_col(C) :- C = #max{Col : input(_, Col, _)}.

% 4-connected adjacency offsets
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

% Non-3 cells on the grid boundary are reachable from outside
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, min_row(Rmin), R = Rmin.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, max_row(Rmax), R = Rmax.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, min_col(Cmin), C = Cmin.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, max_col(Cmax), C = Cmax.

% Flood-fill outward through non-3 cells
outside(R2, C2) :-
    outside(R1, C1), delta(DR, DC),
    R2 = R1+DR, C2 = C1+DC,
    input(R2, C2, V), V != 3.

% Color-3 cells stay 3
:- input(R, C, 3), output(R, C, Color), Color != 3.

% Outside non-3 cells stay 0
:- outside(R, C), output(R, C, Color), Color != 0.

% Interior non-3 cells (enclosed by 3s, not reachable from boundary) become 4
:- input(R, C, 0), not outside(R, C), output(R, C, Color), Color != 4.
</asp>
</example_2>

Let's propose ASP program:

% Determine dimensions
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
n_cols(M) :- M = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% Output grid: each row R (0..N-1) and each output column C (0..2*M-1)
output_cell(R, C) :-
    n_rows(N), n_cols(M),
    R >= 0, R < N,

```
% Determine dimensions
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
n_cols(M) :- M = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% Define that output cells are exactly the cartesian product of input rows and twice the number of columns
output_cell(R, C) :-
    n_rows(N), n_cols(M),
    R >= 0, R < N,
    C >= 0, C < 2*M.

% Colour of an output cell is the colour of the corresponding input cell at (R, C mod M)
output(R, C, Color) :-
    output_cell(R, C),
    C2 = C - M * (C / M),
    input(R, C2, Color).

% Each position must have exactly one colour
:- output(R, C, V1), output(R, C, V2), V1 != V2.

% (optional) No other output cells exist – already satisfied by definition.
```

% Dimensions
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
n_cols(M) :- M = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

    n_rows(N), n_cols(M),

    0 <= C, C < 2*M.

output(R, C, Color) :-
    output_cell(R, C),
    C_mod = C - M * (C / M),
    input(R, C_mod, Color).

% No cell should have two different colours
:- output(R, C, C1), output(R, C, C2), C1 != C2.

<transformation>
The input is treated as a tile map. The output is NxM times larger than the input (where the input is N rows x M cols). Each input cell at (BR, BC) with a non-zero value causes a copy of the full input to be placed in the corresponding NxM block of the output (at rows BR*N .. BR*N+N-1, cols BC*M .. BC*M+M-1). Each input cell with value 0 produces an all-zero block at that position.
</transformation>

<asp>
% Count distinct row/col indices — equals grid dimensions for 0-indexed inputs
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
n_cols(N) :- N = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% Output is NR*NR rows and NC*NC cols (input tiled by itself)
output_cell(R, C) :-
    input(BR, BC, _), input(DR, DC, _),
    n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
    R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC.

1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

% Non-zero block: the sub-cell must equal the input value at the relative position
:- input(BR, BC, V), V != 0,
   input(DR, DC, TileColor),
   n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
   R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC,
   output(R, C, ActualColor), ActualColor != TileColor.

% Zero block: every sub-cell must be 0
:- input(BR, BC, 0),
   input(DR, DC, _),
   n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
   R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC,
   output(R, C, ActualColor), ActualColor != 0.
</asp>
</example_1>

<transformation>
The output is the same size as the input. Color-3 cells are unchanged. Non-3 cells (value 0) that are enclosed by color-3 walls — i.e., cannot reach the grid boundary via 4-connected movement through non-3 cells — are filled with color 4. All other non-3 cells remain 0.
</transformation>

<asp>
% Output has the same dimensions as the input
output_cell(R, C) :- input(R, C, _).
1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

% Grid boundary coordinates
min_row(R) :- R = #min{Row : input(Row, _, _)}.
max_row(R) :- R = #max{Row : input(Row, _, _)}.
min_col(C) :- C = #min{Col : input(_, Col, _)}.
max_col(C) :- C = #max{Col : input(_, Col, _)}.

% 4-connected adjacency offsets
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

% Non-3 cells on the grid boundary are reachable from outside
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, min_row(Rmin), R = Rmin.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, max_row(Rmax), R = Rmax.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, min_col(Cmin), C = Cmin.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, max_col(Cmax), C = Cmax.

% Flood-fill outward through non-3 cells
outside(R2, C2) :-
    outside(R1, C1), delta(DR, DC),
    R2 = R1+DR, C2 = C1+DC,
    input(R2, C2, V), V != 3.

% Color-3 cells stay 3
:- input(R, C, 3), output(R, C, Color), Color != 3.

% Outside non-3 cells stay 0
:- outside(R, C), output(R, C, Color), Color != 0.

% Interior non-3 cells (enclosed by 3s, not reachable from boundary) become 4
:- input(R, C, 0), not outside(R, C), output(R, C, Color), Color != 4.
</asp>
</example_2>

<asp>
% -------------------------------------------------
%  Determine the dimensions of the input grid
% -------------------------------------------------
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
n_cols(M) :- M = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% -------------------------------------------------
%  Build the output grid.
%  Each input cell (R, C2) creates two output cells:
%   • one at column C = C2      (copy 0)
%   • one at column C = C2 + M  (copy 1)
% -------------------------------------------------
output_cell(R, C) :-
    n_rows(N), n_cols(M),
    R >= 0,
    C >= 0,
    R < N,
    C >= 0,
    C < 2*M,
    input(R, C2, _),
    C = C2.

output_cell(R, C) :-
    n_rows(N), n_cols(M),
    R >= 0,
    C >= 0,
    R < N,
    C >= 0,
    C < 2*M,
    input(R, C2, _),
    C = C2 + M.

% -------------------------------------------------
%  The colour of an output cell must be the colour of the
%  source cell (the cell it was copied from).
% -------------------------------------------------
output(R, C, V) :-
    output_cell(R, C),
    input(R, C2, V),
    C = C2.

output(R, C, V) :-
    output_cell(R, C),
    input(R, C2, V),
    C = C2 + M.

% -------------------------------------------------
%  No cell may receive two different colours.
% -------------------------------------------------
:- output(R, C, V1), output(R, C, V2), V1 != V2.

% -------------------------------------------------
%  Enforce that the output contains exactly one colour per
%  position and that the total number of coloured cells is
%  exactly 2*M*N (the size of the expected output grid).
% -------------------------------------------------
#count{ output(R, C, Color) : output(R, C, Color) } = 2*M*N.
</asp>

n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.

% Determine dimensions
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
n_cols(M) :- M = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% Left copy: copy the whole input grid as the left block
output(R, C, V) :-
    input(R, C, V).

% Right copy: copy the whole input grid as the right block shifted by M columns
output(R, C2, V) :-
    input(R, Cb, V),
    C2 = Cb + M.

% Ensure each output cell has exactly one colour
:- output(R, C, V1), output(R, C, V2), V1 != V2.

% Ensure that the number of coloured cells equals twice the number of input cells
#count{output(R, C, Color) : output(R, C, Color)} = 2 * #count{input(R, C, Color)}.

<transformation>
The input is treated as a tile map. The output is NxM times larger than the input (where the input is N rows x M cols). Each input cell at (BR, BC) with a non-zero value causes a copy of the full input to be placed in the corresponding NxM block of the output (at rows BR*N .. BR*N+N-1, cols BC*M .. BC*M+M-1). Each input cell with value 0 produces an all-zero block at that position.
</transformation>

<asp>
% Count distinct row/col indices — equals grid dimensions for 0-indexed inputs
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
n_cols(N) :- N = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% Output is NR*NR rows and NC*NC cols (input tiled by itself)
output_cell(R, C) :-
    input(BR, BC, _), input(DR, DC, _),
    n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
    R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC.

1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

% Non-zero block: the sub-cell must equal the input value at the relative position
:- input(BR, BC, V), V != 0,
   input(DR, DC, TileColor),
   n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
   R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC,
   output(R, C, ActualColor), ActualColor != TileColor.

% Zero block: every sub-cell must be 0
:- input(BR, BC, 0),
   input(DR, DC, _),
   n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
   R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC,
   output(R, C, ActualColor), ActualColor != 0.
</asp>
</example_1>

<transformation>
The output is the same size as the input. Color-3 cells are unchanged. Non-3 cells (value 0) that are enclosed by color-3 walls — i.e., cannot reach the grid boundary via 4-connected movement through non-3 cells — are filled with color 4. All other non-3 cells remain 0.

<asp>
% Output has the same dimensions as the input
output_cell(R, C) :- input(R, C, _).
1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

% Grid boundary coordinates
min_row(R) :- R = #min{Row : input(Row, _, _)}.
max_row(R) :- R = #max{Row : input(Row, _, _)}.
min_col(C) :- C = #min{Col : input(_, Col, _)}.
max_col(C) :- C = #max{Col : input(_, Col, _)}.

% 4-connected adjacency offsets
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

% Non-3 cells on the grid boundary are reachable from outside
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, min_row(Rmin), R = Rmin.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, max_row(Rmax), R = Rmax.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, min_col(Cmin), C = Cmin.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, max_col(Cmax), C = Cmax.

% Flood-fill outward through non-3 cells
outside(R2, C2) :-
    outside(R1, C1), delta(DR, DC),
    R2 = R1+DR, C2 = C1+DC,
    input(R2, C2, V), V != 3.

% Color-3 cells stay 3
:- input(R, C, 3), output(R, C, Color), Color != 3.

% Outside non-3 cells stay 0
:- outside(R, C), output(R, C, Color), Color != 0.

% Interior non-3 cells (enclosed by 3s, not reachable from boundary) become 4
:- input(R, C, 0), not outside(R, C), output(R, C, Color), Color != 4.
</asp>
</example_2>

% -------------------------------------------------
%  Determine the dimensions of the input grid
% -------------------------------------------------
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
n_cols(M) :- M = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% -------------------------------------------------
%  Build the output grid.
%  Each input cell (R, C2) creates two output cells:
%   • one at column C = C2      (copy 0)

output_cell(R, C) :-
    n_rows(N), n_cols(M),
    R >= 0,
    C >= 0,
    R < N,
    C >= 0,
    C < 2*M,

    R < N,
    C >= 0,
    C < 2*M,
    input(R, C2, _),
    C = C2 + M.

output(R, C, V) :-

<asp>
% ------------------------------------------------------------------
% Determine the size of the input grid
% ------------------------------------------------------------------
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
n_cols(M) :- M = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% ------------------------------------------------------------------
% Define the positions of the output grid
% ------------------------------------------------------------------
output_cell(R, C) :-
    n_rows(N),
    n_cols(M),
    R >= 0,
    R < N,
    C >= 0,
    C < 2*M.

% ------------------------------------------------------------------
% Colour of each output cell comes from the corresponding input cell
% ------------------------------------------------------------------
output(R, C, Color) :-
    output_cell(R, C),
    Cmod = C - M * (C / M),      % Cmod = C mod M
    input(R, Cmod, Color).

% ------------------------------------------------------------------
% No cell may contain two different colours (ensures uniqueness)
% ------------------------------------------------------------------
:- output(R, C, V1), output(R, C, V2), V1 != V2.
</asp>

<asp>
% -------------------------------------------------
% Dimensions of the input (optional, used only for the shift)
% -------------------------------------------------
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
n_cols(M) :- M = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% -------------------------------------------------
% Copy the whole input as the left block
% -------------------------------------------------
output(R, C, V) :-
    input(R, C, V).

% -------------------------------------------------
% Copy the whole input as the right block, shifted by M columns
% -------------------------------------------------
output(R, Cb, V) :-
    input(R, Cb0, V),
    Cb = Cb0 + M.

% -------------------------------------------------
% No cell may have two different colours
% -------------------------------------------------
:- output(R, C, V1), output(R, C, V2), V1 != V2.

% -------------------------------------------------
% The output must contain exactly twice as many coloured cells as the input
% -------------------------------------------------
#count{output(R, C, Color) : output(R, C, Color)} = 2 * #count{input(R, C, Color)}.
</asp>

% Determine dimensions
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
n_cols(M) :- M = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

output(R, C, V) :-
    input(R, C, V).

output(R, C2, V) :-
    input(R, C, V),
    M = #count{C0 : input(_, C0, _)},
    C2 = C + M.

:- output(R, C, V1), output(R, C, V2), V1 != V2.

% Optional: ensure total number of output cells is twice number of input cells
#count{R, C : output(R, C, _)} = 2 * #count{R, C : input(R, C, _)}.

% Determine dimensions
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
n_cols(M) :- M = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% Output cells from input cells (left copy)
output(R, C, V) :-
    input(R, C, V).

% Output cells shifted copy (right copy)
output(R, C2, V) :-
    input(R, C, V),
    M = #count{C0 : input(_, C0, _)},
    C2 = C + M.

% No conflicting colors at the same cell
:- output(R, C, V1), output(R, C, V2), V1 != V2.

% Ensure total number of output cells is twice the number of input cells
% Count input cells
IC = #count{R, C : input(R, C, _)}.
% Count output cells
OC = #count{R, C : output(R, C, _)}.
% Enforce count
:- OC = 2 * IC.

<transformation>
The input is treated as a tile map. The output is NxM times larger than the input (where the input is N rows x M cols). Each input cell at (BR, BC) with a non-zero value causes a copy of the full input to be placed in the corresponding NxM block of the output (at rows BR*N .. BR*N+N-1, cols BC*M .. BC*M+M-1). Each input cell with value 0 produces an all-zero block at that position.
</transformation>

<asp>
% Count distinct row/col indices — equals grid dimensions for 0-indexed inputs
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
n_cols(N) :- N = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% Output is NR*NR rows and NC*NC cols (input tiled by itself)
output_cell(R, C) :-
    input(BR, BC, _), input(DR, DC, _),
    n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
    R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC.

1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

% Non-zero block: the sub-cell must equal the input value at the relative position
:- input(BR, BC, V), V != 0,
   input(DR, DC, TileColor),
   n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
   R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC,
   output(R, C, ActualColor), ActualColor != TileColor.

% Zero block: every sub-cell must be 0
:- input(BR, BC, 0),
   input(DR, DC, _),
   n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
   R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC,
   output(R, C, ActualColor), ActualColor != 0.
</asp>
</example_1>

<transformation>
The output is the same size as the input. Color-3 cells are unchanged. Non-3 cells (value 0) that are enclosed by color-3 walls — i.e., cannot reach the grid boundary via 4-connected movement through non-3 cells — are filled with color 4. All other non-3 cells remain 0.
</transformation>

<asp>
% Output has the same dimensions as the input
output_cell(R, C) :- input(R, C, _).
1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

% Grid boundary coordinates
min_row(R) :- R = #min{Row : input(Row, _, _)}.
max_row(R) :- R = #max{Row : input(Row, _, _)}.
min_col(C) :- C = #min{Col : input(_, Col, _)}.
max_col(C) :- C = #max{Col : input(_, Col, _)}.

% 4-connected adjacency offsets
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

% Non-3 cells on the grid boundary are reachable from outside
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, min_row(Rmin), R = Rmin.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, max_row(Rmax), R = Rmax.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, min_col(Cmin), C = Cmin.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, max_col(Cmax), C = Cmax.

% Flood-fill outward through non-3 cells
outside(R2, C2) :-
    outside(R1, C1), delta(DR, DC),
    R2 = R1+DR, C2 = C1+DC,
    input(R2, C2, V), V != 3.

% Color-3 cells stay 3
:- input(R, C, 3), output(R, C, Color), Color != 3.

% Outside non-3 cells stay 0
:- outside(R, C), output(R, C, Color), Color != 0.

% Interior non-3 cells (enclosed by 3s, not reachable from boundary) become 4
:- input(R, C, 0), not outside(R, C), output(R, C, Color), Color != 4.
</asp>
</example_2>

% -------------------------------------------------
%  Determine the dimensions of the input grid
% -------------------------------------------------
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
n_cols(M) :- M = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% -------------------------------------------------
%  Build the output grid.
%  Each input cell (R, C2) creates two output cells:
%   • one at column C = C2      (copy 0)
%   • one at column C = C2 + M  (copy 1)
% -------------------------------------------------
output_cell(R, C) :-
    n_rows(N), n_cols(M),
    R >= 0,
    C >= 0,
    R < N,
    C >= 0,
    C < 2*M,
    input(R, C2, _),
    C = C2.

output_cell(R, C) :-
    n_rows(N), n_cols(M),
    R >= 0,
    C >= 0,
    R < N,
    C >= 0,
    C < 2*M,
    input(R, C2, _),
    C = C2 + M.

% -------------------------------------------------
%  The colour of an output cell must be the colour of the
%  source cell (the cell it was copied from).
% -------------------------------------------------
output(R, C, V) :-
    output_cell(R, C),
    input(R, C2, V),
    C = C2.

output(R, C, V) :-
    output_cell(R, C),
    input(R, C2, V),
    C = C2 + M.

% -------------------------------------------------
%  No cell may receive two different colours.
% -------------------------------------------------
:- output(R, C, V1), output(R, C, V2), V1 != V2.

% -------------------------------------------------
%  Enforce that the output contains exactly one colour per
%  position and that the total number of coloured cells is
%  exactly 2*M*N (the size of the expected output grid).
% -------------------------------------------------
#count{ output(R, C, Color) : output(R, C, Color) } = 2*M*N.
</asp>

% ------------------------------------------------------------------

% ------------------------------------------------------------------
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.

    R < N,
    C >= 0,
    C < 2*M.

% ------------------------------------------------------------------
:- output(R, C, V1), output(R, C, V2), V1 != V2.

<asp>
% -------------------------------------------------
% Dimensions of the input (optional, used only for the shift)
% -------------------------------------------------
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
n_cols(M) :- M = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% -------------------------------------------------
% Copy the whole input as the left block
% -------------------------------------------------
output(R, C, V) :-
    input(R, C, V).

% -------------------------------------------------
% Copy the whole input as the right block, shifted by M columns
% -------------------------------------------------
output(R, Cb, V) :-
    input(R, Cb0, V),
    Cb = Cb0 + M.

% -------------------------------------------------
% No cell may have two different colours
% -------------------------------------------------
:- output(R, C, V1), output(R, C, V2), V1 != V2.

% -------------------------------------------------
% The output must contain exactly twice as many coloured cells as the input
% -------------------------------------------------
#count{output(R, C, Color) : output(R, C, Color)} = 2 * #count{input(R, C, Color)}.
</asp>

<asp>
% Determine the dimensions of the input grid
N = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
M = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% Left copy – copy of the input at the same column
output(R, C, V) :-
    input(R, C, V).

% Right copy – copy of the input shifted by M columns
output(R, C2, V) :-
    input(R, C, V),
    M = #count{C0 : input(_, C0, _)},
    C2 = C + M.

% No two different colours may appear in the same cell
:- output(R, C, V1), output(R, C, V2), V1 != V2.

% Enforce that the output contains exactly twice as many coloured cells as the input
IC = #count{R, C : input(R, C, _)}.
OC = #count{R, C : output(R, C, _)}.
:- OC = 2 * IC.
</asp>

% Determine maximal column index
max_c(Cmax) :- Cmax = #max{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% Determine maximal row index
max_r(Rmax) :- Rmax = #max{R : input(R, _, _)}.

% Number of columns M (1-indexed)
M = Cmax + 1.

% Define cells in the output grid (0-indexed)
cell(R, C) :-
    R >= 0,
    R <= Rmax,
    C >= 0,
    C < 2*M.

% Output for cells that belong to the left half (C < M)
output(R, C, V) :-
    input(R, C, V),
    C >= 0,
    C < M.

% Output for cells that belong to the right half (C >= M)
output(R, C, V) :-
    input(R, C0, V),
    C0 >= 0,
    C0 < M,
    C = C0 + M.

% At most one colour per cell
:- output(R, C, V1), output(R, C, V2), V1 != V2.

% Every cell must have a colour
:- cell(R, C), not output(R, C, _).

% No output outside the defined grid
:- not cell(R, C), not output(R, C, _).

<asp>

% Determine maximal column index
max_c(Cmax) :- Cmax = #max{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% Determine maximal row index
max_r(Rmax) :- Rmax = #max{R : input(R, _, _)}.

% Number of columns M (1-indexed)
M = Cmax + 1.

% Define cells of the output grid
cell(R, C) :-
    R >= 0,
    R <= Rmax,
    C >= 0,
    C < 2*M.

% Output left copy (columns 0..M-1)
output(R, C, V) :-
    input(R, C, V),
    C >= 0,
    C < M.

% Output right copy (columns M..2*M-1)
output(R, C, V) :-
    input(R, C0, V),
    C0 >= 0,
    C0 < M,
    C = C0 + M.

% At most one colour per cell
:- output(R, C, V1), output(R, C, V2), V1 != V2.

% Every cell must have a colour
:- cell(R, C), not output(R, C, _).

% No coloured cell may appear outside the grid
:- not cell(R, C), output(R, C, _).

</asp>

<transformation>
The input is treated as a tile map. The output is NxM times larger than the input (where the input is N rows x M cols). Each input cell at (BR, BC) with a non-zero value causes a copy of the full input to be placed in the corresponding NxM block of the output (at rows BR*N .. BR*N+N-1, cols BC*M .. BC*M+M-1). Each input cell with value 0 produces an all-zero block at that position.
</transformation>

<asp>
% Count distinct row/col indices — equals grid dimensions for 0-indexed inputs
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
n_cols(N) :- N = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% Output is NR*NR rows and NC*NC cols (input tiled by itself)
output_cell(R, C) :-
    input(BR, BC, _), input(DR, DC, _),
    n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
    R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC.

1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

% Non-zero block: the sub-cell must equal the input value at the relative position
:- input(BR, BC, V), V != 0,
   input(DR, DC, TileColor),
   n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
   R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC,
   output(R, C, ActualColor), ActualColor != TileColor.

% Zero block: every sub-cell must be 0
:- input(BR, BC, 0),
   input(DR, DC, _),
   n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
   R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC,
   output(R, C, ActualColor), ActualColor != 0.
</asp>
</example_1>

<transformation>
The output is the same size as the input. Color-3 cells are unchanged. Non-3 cells (value 0) that are enclosed by color-3 walls — i.e., cannot reach the grid boundary via 4-connected movement through non-3 cells — are filled with color 4. All other non-3 cells remain 0.
</transformation>

<asp>
% Output has the same dimensions as the input
output_cell(R, C) :- input(R, C, _).
1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

% Grid boundary coordinates
min_row(R) :- R = #min{Row : input(Row, _, _)}.
max_row(R) :- R = #max{Row : input(Row, _, _)}.
min_col(C) :- C = #min{Col : input(_, Col, _)}.
max_col(C) :- C = #max{Col : input(_, Col, _)}.

% 4-connected adjacency offsets
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

% Non-3 cells on the grid boundary are reachable from outside
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, min_row(Rmin), R = Rmin.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, max_row(Rmax), R = Rmax.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, min_col(Cmin), C = Cmin.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, max_col(Cmax), C = Cmax.

% Flood-fill outward through non-3 cells
outside(R2, C2) :-
    outside(R1, C1), delta(DR, DC),
    R2 = R1+DR, C2 = C1+DC,
    input(R2, C2, V), V != 3.

% Color-3 cells stay 3
:- input(R, C, 3), output(R, C, Color), Color != 3.

% Outside non-3 cells stay 0
:- outside(R, C), output(R, C, Color), Color != 0.

% Interior non-3 cells (enclosed by 3s, not reachable from boundary) become 4
:- input(R, C, 0), not outside(R, C), output(R, C, Color), Color != 4.
</asp>
</example_2>

<asp>
% -------------------------------------------------
%  Determine the maximal row and column indices.
% -------------------------------------------------
max_r(Rmax) :- Rmax = #max{R : input(R, _, _)}.
max_c(Cmax) :- Cmax = #max{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% Number of columns (1‑based)
M = Cmax + 1.

% -------------------------------------------------
%  Cells of the output grid.
%  For a rectangular input the output grid is N rows
%  (rows 0 .. Rmax) and 2*M columns (0 .. 2*M‑1).
% -------------------------------------------------
cell(R, C) :-
    R >= 0,
    R <= Rmax,
    C >= 0,
    C < 2*M.

% -------------------------------------------------
%  Copy of the whole input – two horizontal copies.
% -------------------------------------------------
% left copy (columns 0 … M‑1)
output(R, C, V) :-
    input(R, C, V),
    C >= 0,
    C < M.

% right copy (columns M … 2*M‑1)
output(R, C, V) :-
    input(R, C0, V),
    C0 >= 0,
    C0 < M,
    C = C0 + M.

% -------------------------------------------------
%  At most one colour per cell.
% -------------------------------------------------
:- output(R, C, V1), output(R, C, V2), V1 != V2.

% -------------------------------------------------
%  Every cell inside the output grid must have a colour.
% -------------------------------------------------
:- cell(R, C), not output(R, C, _).

% -------------------------------------------------
%  No coloured cell may appear outside the defined grid.
% -------------------------------------------------
:- not cell(R, C), output(R, C, _).
</asp>

% Determine number of rows and columns
N = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.

:- cell(R, C), not output(R, C, _).

% Ensure that output cells only appear inside defined grid
:- not cell(R, C), output(R, C, _).

% Ensure output grid size matches expectation: total output cells count = N * 2 * M
#count{output(R, C, Color) : output(R, C, Color)} = N * 2 * M.

% Determine number of rows and columns.
max_r(Rmax) :- Rmax = #max{R : input(R, _, _)}.
max_c(Cmax) :- Cmax = #max{C : input(_, C, _)}.
N = max_r + 1.
M = max_c + 1.

% Valid positions in output grid
cell(R, C) :-
    R >= 0,
    R < N,
    C >= 0,
    C < 2*M.

% Left copy: copy input directly (left side)
output(R, C, V) :-
    input(R, C, V),
    C < M.

% Right copy: copy input shifted by M columns
output(R, C, V) :-
    input(R, C0, V),
    C = C0 + M,
    C < 2*M,
    C >= M.

% At most one colour per cell
:- output(R, C, V1), output(R, C, V2), V1 != V2.

% Every cell in the output grid must have a colour (covering)
:- cell(R, C), not output(R, C, _).

% No colour outside the defined grid
:- not cell(R, C), output(R, C, _).

% Ensure that the number of coloured cells matches the expected size (optional)
#count{output(R, C, Color) : output(R, C, Color)} = N * 2 * M.

% Determine maximum row and column indices
max_r(Rmax) :- Rmax = #max{R : input(R, _, _)}.
max_c(Cmax) :- Cmax = #max{C : input(_, C, _)}.
M = Cmax + 1.

% Output cells.
% Left copy (same column)
output(R, C, V) :-
    input(R, C, V).

% Right copy (shifted by M)
output(R, C, V) :-
    input(R, C0, V),
    C = C0 + M.

% At most one colour per cell
:- output(R, C, V1), output(R, C, V2), V1 != V2.

% Each cell of the input grid must have a colour in the output.
:- input(R, C, _), not output(R, C, _).

% No colour outside the input grid
:- not input(R, C, _), output(R, C, _).

% (Optionally) ensure output grid size is exactly twice the input grid (optional)
% Not needed.

% Every cell of the output grid must be coloured

% No colour may appear outside the output grid

% Determine the maximal row and column indices of the input
max_r(Rmax) :- Rmax = #max{R : input(R, _, _)}.
max_c(Cmax) :- Cmax = #max{C : input(_, C, _)}.
M = max_c + 1.                     % number of columns of the input (0‑based)

% Positions of the output grid (rows 0..max_r, columns 0..2*M‑1)
cell(R, C) :-
    R >= 0,
    R <= max_r,
    C >= 0,
    C < 2 * M.

% Copy the whole input unchanged (left block)
output(R, C, V) :-
    input(R, C, V).

% Copy the whole input shifted by M columns (right block)
output(R, C, V) :-
    input(R, C0, V),
    C0 < M,                     % C0 is a column of the input (0..M‑1)
    C = C0 + M.                 % column in the right block (M..2*M‑1)

% No two different colours may appear in the same cell
:- output(R, C, V1), output(R, C, V2), V1 != V2.

% Every cell of the output grid must have exactly one colour
:- cell(R, C), not output(R, C, _).

% No coloured cell may appear outside the defined output grid
:- not cell(R, C), output(R, C, _).
